back_translation: And afterward, when Jesus had finished speaking these words, he departed from the region of Galilee and came into the territory of Judea on the opposite side of the Jordan River.
And large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
Some Pharisees came up to him to test him, asking, "Is it lawful according to Torah-law for a man to divorce his wife for any cause whatsoever?"
He answered, "Have you never read? The Creator made them from the beginning as 'male and female,'
and said, 'For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be bonded as one with his wife, and the two shall become one flesh'?
Therefore they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore, what God has bonded together, let not man separate."
They answered him, "If so, why then did Moses command that a man give his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away?"
He said to them, "Because of the stubbornness of your hearts, Moses permitted you to divorce your wives. But from the beginning it was not so."

I tell you that whoever divorces his wife, not on the ground of sexual immorality, and marries another woman commits adultery. And whoever marries a woman who has been divorced likewise commits adultery."
His disciples said to him, "If the situation between a man and his wife is like this, then it is better not to marry."
He said to them, "Not everyone can accept this word, except those to whom the gift has been given,

for there are eunuchs who were born from their mother's womb that way, and there are eunuchs who were made eunuchs by men, and there are eunuchs who hold themselves as eunuchs for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. Whoever can receive, let him receive."
At that time, people brought little children to him so that he would lay his hands on them and pray for them. But the disciples sternly forbade those people.
But Jesus said, "Let the little children come to me, and do not forbid them, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to those who are like these."
When he had laid his hands on them, he departed from there.
And behold, a man came up to him and asked, "Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?"
He said to him, "Why do you ask me about that which is good? There is only one who is utterly good. If you want to enter into life, keep the commandments."
He asked him, "Which commandments?" Jesus said, "These: 'Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not bear false witness,
honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as yourself.'"
The young man said to him, "All these I have kept. What do I still lack?"
Jesus said to him, "If you want to be complete, go, sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me."
But when the young man heard those words, he went away sorrowful, because he had many possessions.
Then Jesus said to his disciples, "Truly I tell you, a rich person will enter the kingdom of heaven with great difficulty.
I tell you again, it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God."
When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astonished and asked, "If so, who then can be saved?"
Jesus gazed at them and said, "For humans, this is impossible, but for God, all things are possible."
At that time, Peter answered him, "Behold, we have forsaken everything and followed you. So what shall we receive?"

Jesus said to them, "Truly I tell you, in the divine renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on his throne of glory, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
And everyone who has forsaken houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, wife, children, or fields for the sake of my name, that one will receive a hundredfold and will inherit eternal life.
But many who are first will turn out to be last, and many who are last will turn out to be first."
